idea.title: Trail Weaver
idea.hook: Steer two units leaving opposite-color trails — each can only walk on the OTHER's trail; paint 80% of the board without either getting cornered.
idea.prototype: Grid-based two-unit puzzle on hand-shaped boards (open, donut, comb, cross, spiral, hourglass). Each unit drops its color tile each step; can only step onto a tile of the OTHER color (or empty / other-origin). Tap a cell adjacent to the active unit; turns auto-alternate. Goal: 80% painted coverage per level. Lose: any unit has no legal adjacent move (cornered). 6 levels total.
idea.mutation_of: r/WebGames Spark Relay (opposite-color trail puzzle) — synthesised into a two-handed Yandex-portal-scaled puzzle with inverted-dependency steering.
idea.why_novel: Two-unit grid puzzles with INVERTED dependency — each unit literally creates the floor the other walks on — is a mechanic Tim's portfolio has zero coverage of. Sits in the puzzle_sort_match family but is mechanically nothing like merge or match-3.
idea.non_timer_pressure: Resource scarcity (every empty cell is single-use) + inverted dependency (each unit only steps on the OTHER color) + coverage target (80%). No timer anywhere.
idea.lose_condition: Either unit has zero legal adjacent moves on its turn — cornered. State machine flips to GAMEOVER.

sources.title: Trail Weaver (top_score=75.6, verdict=build_now, family=puzzle_sort_match)
sources.snippet: Two-handed simultaneous-control with INVERTED dependency — each unit literally creates the ground the other walks on. Tim's portfolio has zero coverage of this mechanic; Loom Weaver was crafting, Lily Frog was hopping. Trail Weaver introduces a new puzzle genre.

sources.title: Merge Dale
sources.snippet: Reference card for puzzle_objects CTR archetype — large tactile puzzle objects, clear color groups, one obvious almost-solved state. Borrowed object scale + contrast for Trail Weaver cover.

sources.title: Block Dropping Merge
sources.snippet: Second reference card for puzzle_objects archetype — the saturated foreground / quieter background / two-color contrast pattern that reads at thumbnail size.

sources.title: Monster Match
sources.snippet: Tertiary reference — confirmed the readable-at-128x75 / 64x64 thumbnail gate. Trail Weaver cover meets this with two thick ribbons + grid only.

synthesizer_brief: Synthesised from the r/WebGames Spark Relay signal cluster as an inverted-dependency two-unit grid puzzle that fits the puzzle_sort_match Yandex/portal family. Scope-fenced to vanilla JS canvas + 6 hand-shaped levels so it ships in one day.

alternatives.title: Gerrymander
alternatives.hook: Drop 5 city halls on a voter map — Voronoi tessellation redraws every district line, and you must win 6 of 10 districts.
alternatives.why_novel: Voronoi-driven district reshape is genuinely new in casual portals.

alternatives.title: Tile Alchemy
alternatives.hook: Place elemental tiles on a hex map — adjacent tiles fuse into new biomes; brew the one rare biome on today's daily card.
alternatives.why_novel: Hex-fuse alchemy with daily card is a fresher take than standard merge.

alternatives.title: Blink Detective
alternatives.hook: Interrogate a suspect by reading their blink patterns — rapid blinks mean lies, slow blinks mean truth.
alternatives.why_novel: Pattern-reading from face animation is unusual in casual-portal puzzles.

alternatives.title: Body Mortuary
alternatives.hook: Thirty mismatched body parts on the slabs — reassemble the correct ten people from hair, tattoo, and shoe clues.
alternatives.why_novel: Logic-deduction reassembly with morbid theme — fits trending dark-comedy portal slot.

alternatives.title: Throne Verdict
alternatives.hook: Each turn a courtier brings a case; rule mercy or steel — the kingdom shifts under your verdicts.
alternatives.mutation_of: Reigns-likes resurfaced on the synthesis pass
alternatives.why_novel: Verdict-card stack tuned to a one-coffee-break run.
